title: Feature: Stateful Project Tracking Service
description: Crash-resilient persistence for task state, feature progress, and agent lifecycle. When Claude Code crashes or restarts, sessions can pick up where they left off.

steps.description: Add checkpoint domain value objects: TaskSnapshot (serializable snapshot of a SwarmTask with timestamp), FeatureProgress (aggregated progress for a named feature across tiers), and CheckpointEntry (a timestamped envelope wrapping snapshots for a given project). These are pure data types with no behavior beyond construction and serialization.

TaskSnapshot.note: Mirrors SwarmTask but adds snapshotAt timestamp. Imports AgentRole from swarm.ts via re-export in value-objects.

FeatureProgress.note: Aggregated view of a feature's lifecycle progress.

fields: swarmStatus: { topology: SwarmTopology; agentCount: number; status: string }
CheckpointEntry.note: Top-level checkpoint envelope. orphanTasks captures tasks not tied to any feature.

steps.done_condition: bun run check passes, types are importable from domain layer, no circular deps

steps.description: Add ICheckpointPort interface to a new port file. The port defines: checkpoint(entry: CheckpointEntry) => Promise<void> to persist a checkpoint, recover(projectId: string) => Promise<CheckpointEntry | null> to load the latest checkpoint, list(projectId: string) => Promise<CheckpointEntry[]> to list all checkpoints, and prune(projectId: string, keepCount: number) => Promise<number> to remove old checkpoints. The port imports only from domain (checkpoint-types.ts).

modifications.src/core/ports/index.ts: Add re-export: export type { CheckpointEntry, TaskSnapshot, FeatureProgress } from '../domain/checkpoint-types.js'; and export type { ICheckpointPort } from './checkpoint.js';

steps.done_condition: bun run check passes, ICheckpointPort is importable from ports/index.ts, hex analyze shows no boundary violations

steps.description: Implement ICheckpointPort using file-based JSON persistence in .hex/checkpoints/. Each checkpoint is saved as .hex/checkpoints/<projectId>/<timestamp>.json. The recover() method reads the most recent file by sorting filenames. The prune() method deletes all but the N most recent files. Uses IFileSystemPort for all I/O (no direct fs imports). This is the MVP adapter — AgentDB-backed persistence can be swapped in later without changing the port contract.

implementation_notes.error_handling: checkpoint() must not throw on write failure — log to stderr and continue. Recovery failures return null.
implementation_notes.concurrency: Single-writer assumption (one hex process per project). No file locking needed for MVP.
implementation_notes.size_limit: prune() defaults to keeping last 20 checkpoints if keepCount not specified.

steps.done_condition: bun run check passes, unit test for checkpoint/recover round-trip passes, hex analyze shows no boundary violations

steps.description: Create CheckpointOrchestrator use case that: (1) auto-checkpoints when tasks transition status (completed/failed), (2) provides a recover() method that loads the latest checkpoint and returns it for the caller to reconcile, (3) exposes a manualCheckpoint() for explicit saves. The orchestrator composes ICheckpointPort + ISwarmPort — it reads current swarm state, builds a CheckpointEntry, and persists it. It does NOT modify swarm state on recovery; it returns the checkpoint data so the caller (composition root or CLI) decides how to reconcile.

api.constructor: CheckpointOrchestrator(checkpoint: ICheckpointPort, swarm: ISwarmPort, projectId: string)
methods: manualCheckpoint(): Promise<CheckpointEntry> — snapshot current swarm state and persist
recover(): Promise<CheckpointEntry | null> — load latest checkpoint
onTaskTransition(taskId: string, newStatus: SwarmTask['status']): Promise<void> — auto-checkpoint on status change
pruneOld(keepCount?: number): Promise<number> — delegate to port

steps.done_condition: bun run check passes, unit test with mocked ports passes, no boundary violations

steps.description: Wire FileCheckpointAdapter and CheckpointOrchestrator into composition-root.ts. Add checkpointOrchestrator to AppContext type. On startup (non-test), call recover() and log whether a previous checkpoint was found (but do NOT auto-restore — just log for awareness). Register the checkpoint orchestrator so CLI/MCP adapters can trigger manual checkpoints and recovery.

modifications.src/core/ports/app-context.ts: Add checkpointOrchestrator field (import CheckpointOrchestrator type or use the port interface)
modifications.src/composition-root.ts: Import FileCheckpointAdapter + CheckpointOrchestrator, instantiate with projectPath/.hex/checkpoints as basePath, add to returned AppContext, call recover() in non-test startup block

steps.done_condition: bun run build succeeds, bun test passes (existing tests not broken), hex analyze shows no new violations

steps.description: Integration tests for the checkpoint lifecycle: (1) checkpoint/recover round-trip with mock swarm data, (2) prune keeps only N most recent, (3) recover returns null when no checkpoints exist, (4) crash simulation — write checkpoint, simulate process restart by creating new orchestrator instance, verify recover loads previous state. All tests use real FileCheckpointAdapter against a temp directory, mock ISwarmPort.
test_cases: checkpoint() persists a valid JSON file in the expected directory
recover() returns the most recent checkpoint by timestamp
recover() returns null when directory is empty or missing
prune(3) keeps exactly 3 checkpoints and deletes the rest
crash simulation: new orchestrator instance recovers previous checkpoint
CheckpointEntry round-trips through JSON.stringify/parse without data loss
steps.done_condition: bun test passes with all new tests green, no regressions in existing tests

risk_notes: File-based persistence has no atomicity guarantees — a crash mid-write could corrupt the checkpoint file. Mitigation: write to a .tmp file first, then rename (atomic on most filesystems).
CheckpointEntry serialization must handle all SwarmTask status variants — add a round-trip test.
AgentDB adapter swap-in (future) must preserve the same ICheckpointPort contract — design the port methods to be backend-agnostic.
